(0.000 fiat. ii 555.500 fiat) 50.30.21] mmi, “'71“?! aha MINISTRY OF ROAD TRANSPORT AND HIGHWAYS NOTIFICATION New Delhi, the lst January, 2013 SO‘ 405).- Whereas by notification of the Government of India in the erstwhile Ministry of Shipping Road Transport and Highway number S. O. 78 (E), dated the 4'11 February, 1999, 8.0. 1192 (E), dated the 3"1 December, 2001 and 8.0. 920 (E), dated the 28th August, 2002 issued under section 11 of the National Highways Authority of India Act, 1988 (68 of 1988), the Central Government has entrusted the stretch from kilometer 0.000 to kilometer 388.000‘ (Ajmer to Rajasthan / Gujarat Border) of National Highway Number — 8 in the State of Rajasthan, from kilometer 388.000 to kilometer 516.000 (Rajasthan / Gujarat Border to Ahmedabad) of National Highway Number — 8 in the State of Gujarat; from kilometer 114.000 to kilometer 220.000 (Chittorgarh to Udaipur) of National Highway Number 76 and from kilometer 6.000 to kilometer 17.000 (Udaipur Bypass) of National Highway Number — 76 in the State of Rajasthan, from kilometer 14.000 to kilometer 182.000 (Nasirabad to Chittorgarh) of National Highway Number — 79 in the State of Rajasthan and National Highway Number — 79A respectively to the National Highways Authority of India (hereinafter referred to as the “Authority”); And whereas, in exercise of the powers conferred by section 7 of the National Highway Act 1956 (48 of 1956) read with National Highways (Fees for the Use of National Highways Section and Permanent Bridge - Public Funded Project) Rules, 1997, National Highways (Rate of Fee) Rules, 1997 and National Highway Fee (Determination of Rates and Collection) Rules 2008, the Central Government by notification of the Government of India authorized Authority for levying and collecting fee on mechanical vehicles for the use of stretches as follows : (i) Vide $0. No. 2049 (E) dated 23rd August, 2012 for the stretch from km. 0.000 to km. 35.000 on NH-79A and section from km. 15.000 to km. 81.000 on the NH—79 in the State of Rajasthan. -- 7 of 12 -- THE GAZETTE OF INDIA : EXTRAORDINARY [PART H—SEC. 3(ii)]/ (ii) Vide S.O. No. 1783 (E) dated 9th August, 2012 for the stretch from km. 81.000 to km. 163.900 on NH—79 in the State of Rajasthan. (iii) Vide $0. No. 3289 (E) dated 24th December, 2009 for the stretch from km. 159.000 to km 168.500 of NH—79, taking off at km 168.500 of NH-79 and merging at km 213.000 of NH-76 in the State of Rajasthan. , (iv) Vide S.O. No. 1930 (E) dated 21st August, 2012 for the stretch from km. ,’ ' 113.830 to km. 213.000 on NH-76 in the State of Rajasthan. ‘ (v) Vide 5.0. No. 1714 (E) dated 26th July, 2012 for the stretch from km. 278.000 to km. 348.000 on NH—8 in the State of Rajasthan. (Vi) Vide S.O. No. 2623 (E) dated 30Lh October, 2012 for the stretch from km: 348.00 to km. 388.180 on NH—8 in the States of Rajasthan and Gujarat. (vii) Vide S.O. No. 2922 (E) dated 17th December, 2012 for the stretch from km. 388.180 to km. 443000 on NH—8 in the State of Gujarat. , (viii) Vide 8.0. No. 2923 (E) dated 171h December, 2012 for the stretch from km. 443.000 to km. 495.000 on NH—8 in the State of Gujarat. And whereas, pursuant to the provision of section-14 of the National Highways Authority of India Act (68 of 1988) the Authority has entered into an Agreement (hereinafter referred to as said Agreement) with M/s GMR Kishangarh Udaipur Ahmedabad Expressways Limited, hawng its registered office at 4th Floor, Birla Tower, 25, Barakhamba Road, New Delhi — 11001, India (hereinafter referred to as the “Concessionaire”), the successful bidder for bids submitted on 29th July, 2011 for the development of six laning of Kishangarh — Udaipur - Ahmedabad Section from kilometer 0.830 of NH-79A (Design Chainage» 0.000) to kilometer 509.295 of NH—8 (Design Chainage -555.480) (hereinafter referred to as the “said section") in the state of Rajasthan and Gujarat on Design, Build, Finance, Operate and Transfer (DBFOT) basis; Now, therefore, in exercise of the powers conferred by section 8A of the National Highways Act, 1956 (48 of 1956), read with rule 3 of the National Highways Fee (Determination of Rates and Collection) Rules, 2008 published vide G.S.R 838(E) dated 5Lh December, 2008 and amended vide G.S.R 950(E) dated 3ml December, 2010,G.S.R 15(E) dated 12Lh January, 2011 (hereinafter referred to as the “Rules”), and in supersession of the notification bearing No. 3.0. 2049(E) dated 23.08.2012, S.O. 1783(E) dated 09.08.2012, 8.0. 3289 (E) dated 24.12.2009, S.O. 1930(E) dated 21.08.2012, S.O. 1714(E) dated 26.07.2012, 80. 2623(E) dated 30.10.2012, S.O. 2922(E) dated 17.12.2012, and 8.0. 2923(E) dated 17.12.2012 except as respects things done or omitted to be done before such supersession, the Central Government, having regard to the expenditure involved in building, maintenance, management and operation of the said section, interest on the capital invested, reasonable return, the volume of traffic and the period of the Agreement entered into between the Authority and the Concessionaire, hereby notifies that there shall be levied and collected fees on mechanical vehicles for the use of the section from km. 0.830 to km. 36.100 of NH—79A, from km. 15.000 to km 164.000 (start of existing Chittorgarh bypass), existing Chittorgarh bypass (29.600 km) of NH—79, from km 213.000 of NH-76 (end of existing Chittorgarh bypass) to km 117.500 (start of proposed Udaipur bypass), proposed Udaipur bypass (24.500 km) of NH—76 and from " km 287.000 (end of proposed Udaipur bypass) to km 509.295 (existing Chainage) of NH—8 near Ahmedabad i.e. from km. 0.000 to km. 555.480 of the design chainage at the rate specified in columns (2) of the Table 1 for net road section 531.66 km for use of the four and more lane sections (hereinafter referred to as “base rate”) and at the base rates specified in the Table No.2 for bridges/structures costing Rupees -- 8 of 12 -- [am 11.43": 3(ii)] W 3-7! m : WW Fifty Crore or more and at one and half times the base rate for bypasses costing Rupees ten crore or more on the Type of Vehicles specified in Table 1 and 2 below and authorizes the said Concessionaire to collect and retain the fee on and from the date of commercial operation or publication of this notification in the Official Gazette, whichever is later, till the termination date as specified in the agreement. Table 1 ‘ Base rate of fee per km for the hue year - Type of vehicle 2007-08 (in Rupees) (1) (2) Car, Jeep, Van or Light Motor Vehicle 0.65 Light Commercial Vehicle, Light Goods Vehicle or Mini Bus 1.05 Bus or Truck (Two Axles) 2.20 Three-axle commercial vehicles 2.40 Heavy Construction Machinery (l-ICMl or Earth Moving 3.45 Equipment (EME) or Multi Axle Vehicle (MAV) (four to six axles) Oversized Vehicles (seven or more axles] 4.20 , Table 2 “Base rate of fee Ru ees r vehicle r tri Light C313 Jeep. For every additional Rupees five Crore or part thereof, exceeding Rupees one 0.75 1.15 2.25 2.45 3.40 4.50 hundred Crore and upto Rupees two hundred Crore. For every additional Rupees five Crore or part thereof, 0.50 0.75 1.50 1.65 2.25 3 exceeding Rupees two hundred Crore. Notes: 1. The fee levied and collected hereunder shall be due and payable at the following Toll Plaza for the distance specified for such Toll Plaza(s): é a C} n — x -- 9 of 12 -- 10 THE GAZETTE OF INDIA : EXTRAORDINARY [Pm Il—SEC. 301)] V _ A. During Construction Period: Location of Toll Plaza(s) Existing (Design) Chainage Length (in km) for which Fee is payable At km 40.75 on NH-79 (km 60.500), near village Bandanwara in Ajmer district in the State of Rajasthan 90.50 At km 100.750 on NH-79 (km 120.500), near village Larnbia in Bhilwara district in the State of Rajasthan ‘1 3 61.84 At km 163.900 on NH—79 (km 184. 170), near village Jojro Ka Kheda in Chittorgarh district in the State of Rajasthan 70.13 At km 165.800 on NH—76 [km 260.755), near village Narayanpura in Udaipur district in the State of Rajasthan 90.84 At km 348.450 on NH-8 (km 394.185), near village Khandi Obn' in Udaipur district in the State of Rajasthan 103.75 At km 415.500 on NH—8 (km 461.685), near village Vantada in Sabarkantha district in the State of Gujarat 62.00 At km 472.200 on NH-8 (km 518.185), near village Kathpur in Gandhinagar district in the State of Gujarat 52.71 B. After completion of six laning: Location of 'I‘oll Plaza(s) Existing (Design) Chainage Length (in km) for which Fee is payable At km 40.75 on NH—79 (km 60.500), near village . . . . . 90.50 Bandanwara 1n Ajmer district in the State of Rajasthan At km 100.750 on NH—79 (km 120.500), near village Lambia in Bhilwara district in the State 61.84 of Rajasthan -- 10 of 12 -- [um II~1§E 3(ii)] qua an m : mam ll At km 163.900 on NH-79 (km 184.170), near village Jojro Ka Kheda in Chittorgarh district in the State of Rajasthan 70.13 At km 165.800 on NH-76 (km 260.755), near village Narayanpura in Udaipur district in the State of Rajasthan 86.94 At km 348.450 on NH-8 (km 394.185), near village Khandi 0in in Udaipur district in the State of Rajasthan 94.75 At km 415.500 on NH—8 (km 461.685), near village Vantada . in Sabarkantha district in the . State of Gujarat 62.00 At km 472.200 on NH-8 (km ' 518.185), near village Kathpur in Gandhinagar district in the State of Gujarat 65.50 2. In addition to above, the following fee shall also be due and payable for the use of the following bypass(es) at the following toll plaza(s) at one and half times of the base rate for the length of bypass(es) specified for such toll plaza(s)( after completion of Bypass): Chainage Location of Toll Plaza(s) Existing (Design) Length of Bypass (in km) for which additional Fee is payable At km 14.500 on Udaipur bypass( km 323.875), near village Dakan Kotada in Udaipur district in the state of Rajasthan. 22.76 3. In addition to above, the following fee shall also be due and payable for the use of the following bridge(s)/structure(s), cost of which is specified hereunder, at the following Toll Plaza(s) ( after completion of Bypass): Location of ’I‘oll Plaza(s) Existing (Design) Chainage Length (in km) and Cost of bridge /structure (in Rupees Crore) for which additional fee is payable Length Cost At km 14.500 on Udaipur bypass( km 323.875), near village Dakan Kotada in Udaipur district in the state of 1.02 km 140.64 Rajasthan. 4. As regards bridges and bypasses costing Rs. 50 crore or more and costing Rs. 10 crore or more respectively as specified in Note 2 and 3 above, the fee levied shall be due and payable only after completion of such bypasses and structures. -- 11 of 12 -- 12 THE GAZETTE OF INDIA : EXTRAORDINARY [pm II—Sac. 3(ii)] 5. The rate of monthly pass for a person who owns a mechanical vehicle registered for non-commercial purposes and resides within a distance of twenty kilometers from the toll plaza is Rs. 200.00 (Rupees Two Hundred only) for the year 2012-13 which is subject to revision every year as per the provisions of the National Highways Fee (Determination of Rates and Collection) Rules, 2008. 6. For multiple journeys on following rates, namely. ' the highway section, passes shall be issued at the Amount Payable Maximum number of one way journeys allowed Period of validity One and half times of the fee for one way journey Two Twenty four hours from the time of payment TWO-third of amount of the fee payable for fifty or more single journeys Fifty or more One month from date of payment 7. Fee for commercial vehicle (excluding vehicles plying under national permit) registered in the district where the fee plaza falls shall be 50% of the prescribed rate for that category of vehicle provided no service road or alternative road is available for use of such commercial vehicles. 8. Mechanical vehicles which is loaded in excess of permissible load specified for its category under the Rules, shall be liable to pay fee at such rates which is applicable for the next higher category of the vehicles and shall not be entitled to make use of the highway section unless the excess load has been removed from such mechanical vehicle. 9. All definitions including category of mechanical vehicles, its permissible load, category of exempted vehicles, service road, alternative road etc. shall be as per definitions specified in the Rules. 10. 11. The fee rates shall be displayed for information of the users in accordance with rules 12 of the said Rules. ‘ Based on the Base rate of fee per km for the base year 2007 ~08 as mentioned in Table-1 85 2, the actual amount of fee to be charged from the mechanical vehicles and the discounts will be calculated by the Authority and revised annually in accordance with the rule 5 of the said rules provided that no revision shall be effected within six months and the same shall be published in at least one newspaper, each in English and vernacular language accordingly. [F.Nn.NHAl/13013/686/CMD-C0/1l-12/GC Udaipur (Km 0.000 to Km 555.500) BOT] ALKESH SHARMA, n. Secy.
